schema.appName: New vs Old Tax Regime Comparison Calculator
schema.appDescription: Compare income tax under new regime vs old regime for FY 2026-27. Instantly see which saves more tax based on your deductions.

howToSteps.name: Enter your details
howToSteps.text: Fill in the input fields for the New vs Old Tax Regime Calculator. All inputs have smart defaults.

howToSteps.name: View instant results
howToSteps.text: Results update in real time as you type — no submit button needed.

howToSteps.name: Explore breakdown
howToSteps.text: Open the year-by-year breakdown table to see how your numbers change over time.

howToSteps.name: Compare scenarios
howToSteps.text: Adjust the inputs to compare different scenarios and find the best option for your situation.

faq.q: Which tax regime is better for salaried employees in 2026-27?
faq.a: The new regime is default from FY 2024-25 and benefits those with low deductions. The old regime benefits those with large 80C investments (PPF, ELSS, LIC), HRA claims, home loan interest, and NPS contributions. The crossover point is typically around ₹3–4 lakh in total deductions for incomes between ₹10–15 lakh.

faq.q: Can I switch between old and new regime every year?
faq.a: Salaried individuals can switch between old and new regime each financial year. Business owners/self-employed can switch to old regime only once and cannot switch back. Make the choice before the financial year start by informing your employer for TDS purposes.

faq.q: What deductions are not available under the new tax regime?
faq.a: The new regime disallows: HRA exemption, LTA, Section 80C (PPF, ELSS, LIC), 80D (health insurance), home loan interest deduction, NPS 80CCD(1B), and most other Chapter VI-A deductions. Only ₹75,000 standard deduction and employer NPS contribution are allowed.

faq.q: Is the New vs Old Regime Calculator free?
faq.a: Yes, the New vs Old Regime Calculator on CalcPhi is completely free with no registration required.
No. All calculations run in your browser. We do not store any financial data.

faq.q: Can I use the New vs Old Regime Calculator on mobile?
faq.a: Yes. CalcPhi is fully mobile-optimised and works on all modern smartphones.

examples.heading: ₹12 lakh salary with full 80C and HRA
examples.text: For ₹12 lakh salary with ₹1.5 lakh 80C and ₹1.2 lakh HRA exemption, old regime tax ≈ ₹78,000; new regime tax ≈ ₹nil (rebate u/s 87A). At ₹12 lakh income, new regime wins due to full rebate up to ₹12 lakh.

examples.heading: ₹20 lakh salary — comparing regimes
examples.text: For ₹20 lakh salary with ₹1.5 lakh 80C + ₹1.5 lakh HRA + ₹50,000 NPS, old regime tax ≈ ₹2.73 lakh; new regime tax ≈ ₹2.63 lakh. New regime is marginally better here, but close — check with exact deductions using our calculator.
